display.purpose: Homepage hero only. The system's loudest type.

headline.purpose: Dominant section titles across the site.

body.purpose: Paragraphs, descriptions, long-form copy. Capped at 72ch.

label.purpose: Buttons, form labels, badge text. Never below 14px.

shadows.name: signal-glow
shadows.purpose: Signature elevation. Appears on primary button hover and active focus states. The brand's only decorative shadow.

shadows.name: shadow-sm
shadows.purpose: Subtle separation for sticky headers and toolbars.

shadows.name: shadow-md
shadows.purpose: Cards on hover, dropdown menus, popovers.

shadows.name: shadow-lg
shadows.purpose: Modals, command palettes, the rare element that needs to dominate.

shadows.name: focus-ring-input
shadows.purpose: Form input focus state. Carries the elevation language consistent with buttons.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: The entire motion vocabulary. Exponential ease-out for every transition. No bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: duration-interactive
motion.purpose: Default interactive transition speed. Buttons, links, inputs, hover states.

motion.name: duration-page-transition
motion.purpose: Page-level view transitions (theme wipe). Slightly longer than interactive to read as deliberate, not jarring.

motion.name: translate-press
motion.purpose: Primary button hover translate. The entire interaction language. No scale, no rotate, no gradient sweep.

motion.name: typing-cursor-blink
motion.purpose: Scoped utility for purposeful 'terminal' moments only. Pure CSS step-end blink, never used for sequenced typing intros.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The signal-cyan CTA. Used for the main action on every page (Book a Call, Start Free Audit, Get Started).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; font-family: 'Satoshi', 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.02em; padding: 0.75rem 1.5rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; background: #22d3ee; color: #0a0b0f; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), box-shadow 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #06b6d4; transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: 0 0 24px rgba(34, 211, 238, 0.15); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #22d3ee; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Primary Button (Large)
components.description: Hero-scale primary CTA. Same visual language as Primary Button, larger padding and radius for hero placements.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary-lg { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; font-family: 'Satoshi', 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.02em; padding: 1rem 2rem; border-radius: 0.75rem; background: #22d3ee; color: #0a0b0f; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), box-shadow 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-primary-lg:hover { background: #06b6d4; transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: 0 0 24px rgba(34, 211, 238, 0.15); } .ds-btn-primary-lg:active { transform: translateY(0); } .ds-btn-primary-lg:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #22d3ee; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: The secondary CTA. Used alongside Primary Button when the page offers two paths (e.g. Hero: 'Audit' primary + 'Book a Call' outline).
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; font-family: 'Satoshi', 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.02em; padding: 0.75rem 1.5rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; background: transparent; color: #e4e6ec; border: 1px solid #2a2d38; cursor: pointer; transition: color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover { border-color: #22d3ee; color: #22d3ee; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-outline:active { transform: translateY(0); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #22d3ee; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Form input for audit form, contact form, automation-box configurator. Visible labels above the field; placeholder is never the label.
components.css: .ds-input-label { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem; font-family: 'Satoshi', 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 500; color: #e4e6ec; } .ds-input-field { font-family: 'Satoshi', 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; border: 1.5px solid #2a2d38; border-radius: 0.5rem; background: #1e2028; color: #e4e6ec; transition: border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), box-shadow 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-input-field::placeholder { color: #5a5e6e; } .ds-input-field:focus { outline: none; border-color: #22d3ee; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(34, 211, 238, 0.15); }

components.name: Hero Command Panel (Signature)
components.description: The 'Live orchestration' step list in the hero. The system's signature component — embodies the brand. Active step has a signal-cyan left-edge accent. Do not re-skin as a horizontal stepper.
components.html:
Live orchestrationCommand stack online
01Lead captured and enriched
02OpenClaw routes next action
03Proposal drafted and delivered
04Operator notified only if needed
components.css: .ds-command-panel { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.75rem; padding: 1.5rem; background: #181a21; border: 1px solid #2a2d38; border-radius: 0.75rem; font-family: 'Satoshi', 'Inter', sans-serif; color: #e4e6ec; max-width: 28rem; } .ds-command-head { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; padding-bottom: 0.75rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #1f2230; font-size: 0.8125rem; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-command-status { color: #22d3ee; font-weight: 600; } .ds-command-latency { color: #8b8f9e; } .ds-command-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.375rem; font-size: 0.9375rem; color: #8b8f9e; transition: color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-command-item.ds-is-active { color: #e4e6ec; background: rgba(34, 211, 238, 0.06); border-left: 2px solid #22d3ee; padding-left: 0.625rem; } .ds-command-step { font-family: 'Cabinet Grotesk', 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: #5a5e6e; min-width: 1.5rem; } .ds-command-item.ds-is-active .ds-command-step { color: #22d3ee; }

components.name: Service Card
components.description: Service offering card. Used in the 'Automation that actually works' section grid. Full borders, never side-stripes. Nested cards forbidden.
components.html:
Lead Enrichment Pipelines

Pull from Perplexity, score in Claude, drop into your CRM. We architect the whole flow.

See how it works
components.css: .ds-service-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 1rem; padding: 1.5rem; background: #111318; border: 1px solid #2a2d38; border-radius: 0.75rem; transition: border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-service-card:hover { border-color: #22d3ee; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-service-card-title { font-family: 'Cabinet Grotesk', 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #e4e6ec; line-height: 1.15; margin: 0; } .ds-service-card-body { font-family: 'Satoshi', 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #8b8f9e; margin: 0; } .ds-service-card-link { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; font-family: 'Satoshi', 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; font-weight: 500; color: #22d3ee; text-decoration: none; transition: gap 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); margin-top: auto; } .ds-service-card-link:hover { gap: 0.75rem; }

components.name: Section Title Pair
components.description: Section title + description. The two travel together as a unit — never ship one without the other. Marks the start of every page section.
components.html:
Automation that actually works

Production AI workflows for sales handoffs, support triage, reporting, and back-office execution. Real systems, not slideware.
components.css: .ds-section-header { margin-bottom: 3rem; max-width: 60ch; } .ds-section-title { font-family: 'Cabinet Grotesk', 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif; font-size: clamp(2rem, 1.2rem + 2.5vw, 3.5rem); font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: -0.02em; line-height: 1.1; color: #e4e6ec; margin: 0 0 1rem 0; } .ds-section-desc { font-family: 'Satoshi', 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #8b8f9e; margin: 0; max-width: 60ch; }

components.name: Theme Toggle (with view transition)
components.description: Sun/moon icon button that toggles data-theme on :root. Click animates a circular clip-path reveal from cursor coordinates over 220ms with system ease-out. Falls back to instant toggle when startViewTransition is unsupported. The brand's only signature page-level transition.
components.html: <button class="ds-theme-toggle" aria-label="Switch theme"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="5"/><path d="M12 1v2M12 21v2M4.22 4.22l1.42 1.42M18.36 18.36l1.42 1.42M1 12h2M21 12h2M4.22 19.78l1.42-1.42M18.36 5.64l1.42-1.42"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-theme-toggle { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; padding: 0; background: transparent; border: none; color: #e4e6ec; cursor: pointer; border-radius: 0.5rem; transition: color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-theme-toggle:hover { color: #22d3ee; } .ds-theme-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #22d3ee; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Typing Cursor (scoped utility)
components.description: Pure-CSS blink primitive for purposeful 'terminal' moments only. Apply after a single static phrase. Never use for sequenced typing intros, decoration, or ambient noise.
components.css: .ds-cmd-line { font-family: 'Satoshi', 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 0.9375rem; color: #22d3ee; letter-spacing: 0.04em; } .ds-typing-cursor { display: inline-block; width: 2px; height: 1em; background: #22d3ee; margin-left: 2px; vertical-align: text-bottom; animation: ds-blink 0.7s step-end infinite; } @keyframes ds-blink { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: 0; } }

narrative.overview: A3 Lab's site IS what A3 Lab sells: a calm, organized control surface that runs the unsexy ops layer of a business so the team can focus on higher-leverage work. Every design decision asks one question — does this feel like a senior operator's terminal, or a marketing site cosplaying as one? The system is dark by default, surfaces are flat at rest, and elevation comes from a single signature glow: signal cyan radiating around active states. Type pairs Cabinet Grotesk display over Satoshi body — confident, deliberate, slightly literary, never marketing-bro. Color is restrained: 90% neutrals + tinted surfaces, signal cyan reserved for primary action and live state, operator amber held for human-in-the-loop moments. Quiet is louder than loud here. We compete against agencies that scream by refusing to.
keyCharacteristics: Dark by default, light as parity
90/10 color: neutrals dominate, signal cyan + operator amber are scarce by design
Flat surfaces + signal-color glow as the only elevation signature
Cabinet Grotesk display over Satoshi body
Generous spacing scale; the system breathes
Motion is exponential ease-out at 180ms — present but never showy
Every visual decision earns its place; restraint is the brand

rules.name: The 10% Rule
rules.body: Signal Cyan + Operator Amber together occupy ≤10% of any given screen. Their rarity is the entire point. When you find yourself coloring three things the same accent, two of them are wrong.

rules.name: The No-Gradient Rule
rules.body: No background-clip gradient text, ever. The current site has gradient-text on a few headings — those are debt to be repaid, not a pattern to extend. Emphasis comes from weight, scale, and color contrast — not from a rainbow on the headline.

rules.name: The Tinted-Black Rule
rules.body: Every neutral is tinted toward the cyan hue, not pure greyscale. Pure #000 and pure #fff are forbidden.

rules.name: The One-H1 Rule
rules.body: Each page gets exactly one H1, sized at the Hero or Display step. Multiple H1s dilute the page's claim about what it's for.

rules.name: The 72ch Rule
rules.body: Body copy never exceeds 72 characters per line. This is a readability floor, not a style suggestion.

rules.name: The Tracking-Tight Rule
rules.body: Display/Headline/Title all sit at slightly negative letter-spacing. This is what keeps Cabinet Grotesk from looking sparse at large sizes.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows appear only as a response to state (hover, active, elevated above scroll), never as decoration on a static element.

rules.name: The Glow-Is-Signal Rule
rules.body: The signal-cyan glow on :hover and :focus-visible is the brand's elevation signature. It carries the 'live, active, online' meaning that maps to the product. Don't dilute it with secondary glows on other accent colors.

rules.name: The Reduced-Motion-At-Definition Rule
rules.body: Every animation must have a paired @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override declared at the same point in the source as the animation itself, not retrofitted later. The pattern in effects.css §4 is the template — animation block, then immediate paired reduced-motion override. Audit any new motion against this discipline at PR review.

dos: Do keep the 90/10 ratio: 90% tinted neutrals, 10% combined signal cyan + operator amber.
Do use signal cyan for primary actions and live state only.
Do use operator amber for human-in-the-loop moments specifically — approval gates, audit indicators, friction-by-design moments.
Do lead every section with the section-title + section-description pair. They travel together.
Do keep elevation flat at rest. The signal-cyan glow on hover is the only signature lift the system has.
Do ship prefers-reduced-motion respect on every new animation.
Do keep focus-visible rings at the existing 2px signal-cyan / 3px offset minimum.
Do verify cyan + amber distinguishability across deuteranopia and protanopia.
Do keep body line length capped at 72ch and labels at 14px floor.
Do use Cabinet Grotesk display only; if you reach for a third typeface, you're patching a problem the existing pairing should solve.
donts: Don't use background-clip: text gradients on any heading. The current site has a few — those are debt, not a pattern.
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-left or border-right >1px as a colored accent on cards, list items, callouts).
Don't use the hero-metric template (huge number + tiny label + supporting stat row). It's a SaaS landing-page cliche.
Don't ship identical 3-card grids with icon + heading + 2-line description repeated endlessly.
Don't wrap cards inside cards. Ever.
Don't use glassmorphism — backdrop-filter blur as decoration.
Don't use modals as the first thought. Exhaust inline and progressive alternatives first.
Don't drift into generic AI-agency territory: purple/cyan radial gradients on dark, neon cyber grids, robot mascots in hero, 'unleash the power of AI' copy.
Don't drift into corporate-consultancy territory: navy + grey palettes, stock photos of diverse-people-in-suits, 'thought leadership' boilerplate.
Don't drift into marketing-bro territory: orange-arrow emojis, urgency stacking, countdown timers, 'crushing it' / 'game-changer' / 'no-brainer' copy.
Don't use pure #000 or pure #fff. Every neutral tints toward the brand hue.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing. Exponential ease-out at 180ms is the entire motion vocabulary.
Don't animate CSS layout properties (width, height, margin). Animate transform and opacity only.
Don't use em dashes. Use commas, colons, semicolons, or parentheses.
